reasons: The program already produces multiple clear_domain_chip candidates.
Acceptance rate is high enough to justify a larger intake pass.

accepted_candidates.label: Security Control Crosswalk Copilot
accepted_candidates.description: Builds and maintains a reusable control-and-evidence crosswalk across SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, and similar frameworks.
accepted_candidates.specialization_surface: mapping one internal control baseline to multiple external framework requirements
accepted_candidates.mastery_surface: reducing repeated evidence collection by learning reusable control crosswalks and evidence mappings
accepted_candidates.user_value_loop: ingest a new framework request, map it to the control baseline, attach reusable evidence, and refine the crosswalk after auditor or customer review
accepted_candidates.evidence_summary: Hyperproof says teams repeat administrative work when they map frameworks one at a time and re-collect evidence, while ControlMap and practitioner discussion describe one control mapped to many frameworks with evidence reuse as the operational win.
accepted_candidates.raw_observation: Security and GRC teams keep rebuilding the same control-to-framework mapping and evidence matrix instead of operating a maintained crosswalk.
classification_reasons: Has domain shape, specialization surface, mastery surface, and evidence summary.

accepted_candidates.label: Vendor Assertion Evidence Verifier
accepted_candidates.description: Requests, validates, and tracks supporting artifacts behind vendor questionnaire claims for high-risk reviews.
accepted_candidates.specialization_surface: turning self-reported questionnaire answers into verified evidence packets for vendor risk teams
accepted_candidates.mastery_surface: learning which artifact types, freshness windows, and follow-ups actually resolve high-risk vendor assertions
accepted_candidates.user_value_loop: parse vendor answers, request supporting evidence, verify artifact freshness and scope, and update the vendor evidence profile for future reviews
accepted_candidates.evidence_summary: UpGuard and Safe Security both describe repetitive questionnaires, inconsistent responses, and verification difficulty as core TPRM bottlenecks, and Safe Security explicitly recommends requesting recent supporting artifacts for critical control assertions instead of trusting questionnaire answers alone.
accepted_candidates.raw_observation: Buyer-side security teams still spend manual cycles turning vendor attestations into evidence they can trust, especially for critical vendors.
classification_reasons: Has domain shape, specialization surface, mastery surface, and evidence summary.

accepted_candidates.label: Trust Center Gap Copilot
accepted_candidates.description: Prepares the exact missing documents, report access workflows, and follow-up answers needed when a trust center does not fully satisfy a customer security review.
accepted_candidates.specialization_surface: closing the gap between reusable trust-center content and bespoke customer security review asks
accepted_candidates.mastery_surface: learning which trust-center gaps recur and how to answer them with minimal manual pre-sales and security effort
accepted_candidates.user_value_loop: detect repeated customer asks, point to existing trust-center assets, package missing evidence, and update reusable trust-center content after each review
accepted_candidates.evidence_summary: Cyberday positions trust centers as a way to cut repetitive security reviews, and practitioners still report that customers ask for the same evidence in different formats, leaving a recurring gap-handling layer after the trust center is in place.
accepted_candidates.raw_observation: Even after SOC 2 and a trust center are in place, teams still need a repeatable way to handle the leftover spreadsheet, portal, and evidence-gap work.
classification_reasons: Has domain shape, specialization surface, mastery surface, and evidence summary.

accepted_candidates.label: Shared Responsibility Matrix Copilot
accepted_candidates.description: Builds and maintains customer-specific shared responsibility matrices for cloud, managed security, and compliance reviews.
accepted_candidates.specialization_surface: mapping provider-versus-customer ownership for security controls in review and audit contexts
accepted_candidates.mastery_surface: learning recurring responsibility splits, evidence needs, and customer misunderstandings across shared-control reviews
accepted_candidates.user_value_loop: ingest the service boundary, generate the control ownership matrix, attach supporting rationale, and refine the matrix after each customer or auditor challenge
accepted_candidates.evidence_summary: Summit 7, KTL Solutions, and the Cloud Security Alliance all describe shared-responsibility matrices as a recurring requirement in cloud compliance work, where teams must repeatedly clarify which party owns which control and update that mapping as the engagement changes.
accepted_candidates.raw_observation: Security teams keep rebuilding ownership matrices for the same cloud and managed-service controls whenever a customer, auditor, or regulator asks who is actually responsible.
classification_reasons: Has domain shape, specialization surface, mastery surface, and evidence summary.

accepted_candidates.label: Compensating Control Justification Copilot
accepted_candidates.description: Documents, packages, and maintains auditor-ready compensating control narratives when standard controls cannot be met directly.
accepted_candidates.specialization_surface: turning security gaps into documented compensating-control packages with rationale, evidence, and review trails
accepted_candidates.mastery_surface: learning which alternative safeguards, risk arguments, and review cadences actually satisfy auditors and customer reviewers
accepted_candidates.user_value_loop: detect a control gap, draft the compensating-control package, attach validation evidence, and update the justification after review or environmental change
accepted_candidates.evidence_summary: Fortinet describes compensating controls as context-specific alternatives that need clear rationale, validation, and ongoing review, while Security Info Watch notes that teams rely on them heavily but often fail to verify they still mitigate the intended risk.
accepted_candidates.raw_observation: Teams repeatedly need to explain why an alternative safeguard is sufficient, show that it addresses the original risk, and keep that justification current for auditors and customer reviews.
classification_reasons: Has domain shape, specialization surface, mastery surface, and evidence summary.

accepted_candidates.label: Vendor Reassessment Delta Copilot
accepted_candidates.description: Tracks what changed between vendor security reviews, identifies stale artifacts, and prepares the delta package for reassessment.
accepted_candidates.specialization_surface: comparing a new vendor reassessment against the prior evidence set, scores, and control assertions
accepted_candidates.mastery_surface: learning which vendor changes, artifact ages, and reassessment triggers actually matter for ongoing third-party risk decisions
accepted_candidates.user_value_loop: ingest prior assessment data, detect material changes or stale evidence, request the smallest missing delta set, and update the vendor review baseline after each reassessment
accepted_candidates.evidence_summary: SAFE’s questionnaire and TPRM guidance says high-risk vendors need periodic reassessment, recent supporting artifacts, and trigger-based review after major changes, which creates a recurring delta-analysis workflow rather than a one-time questionnaire task.
accepted_candidates.raw_observation: Third-party risk teams keep rechecking the same vendors and mainly need to know what changed, what evidence is stale, and whether a new event should force a deeper reassessment.
classification_reasons: Has domain shape, specialization surface, mastery surface, and evidence summary.

next_actions: Feed the clear_domain_chip set into a smaller MiroFish evaluation run.
The current pilot is strong enough to justify a 250-agent scale pass.
